narrative:
Karnataka High Court heard **879 of 2,794 listed matters**, an overall hearing rate of **31.5%**. That is a clear slowdown from the available recent context: **43.2% on 2026-04-23**, **38.9% on 2026-04-22**, and **38.0% on 2026-04-17**. The court also carried a heavier list today than yesterday (2,794 vs 2,200 scheduled), but still disposed of fewer matters in absolute terms (879 vs 950), indicating weaker list conversion rather than just higher volume.

Judge-wise, the strongest high-volume performances came from **V Srishananda (61/87, 70.1%)**, **M.I. Arun (39/54, 72.2%)**, **R. Nataraj (34/50, 68.0%)**, **K.S. Hemalekha (57/100, 57.0%)**, and **P. Sree Sudha (49/88, 55.7%)**. **Jayant Banerji & Rajesh Rai K** also converted **19 of 22 matters (86.4%)**. On the weaker side, some large rosters sharply reduced court-wide throughput: **S.G. Pandit & K.V. Aravind heard only 1 of 358 matters (0.3%)** on one board, while **Anant Ramanath Hegde** had one list at **1 of 98 (1.0%)** and **H.P. Sandesh** heard **0 of 16**. Notably, **V Srishananda** has also appeared among the top performers in the recent historical snapshots, so today’s strong conversion is consistent rather than isolated.

Stage data shows the main constraint was **Orders**, the largest bucket at **1,215 matters**, but with only **294 heard (24.2%)**. That is far below **48.5% yesterday** and well below **39.6% on 2026-04-22**. **Admission (43.1%)** and **Unknown (42.5%)** converted best among major stages, while **Final Hearing (26.7%)** and **Further Hearing (28.0%)** remained slow. By case type, **WP** dominated volume (**922 scheduled, 336 heard, 36.4%**) and held up reasonably, but appellate categories were the clearest drag: **WA** was only **23 of 443 (5.2%)** and **CA** just **8 of 168 (4.8%)**. In contrast, **CRL.RP (69.1%)**, **MFA (43.8%)**, and **RFA (42.4%)** showed materially better clearance.
